ALL SPECIALTIES / DENTAL ASSISTANT / KENTUCKY

Photo: Unsplash / Unsplash

Kentucky Dental Assistant Registration

Kentucky Dental Assistant license requirements, fees, and steps.

Last updated 2026-03-22 · Sources verified

  • New Applicant
  • Reciprocity
  • Renewal

New Applicant

Dental Assistant applications in Kentucky run through Kentucky Board of Dentistry. Use the checklist below before starting your application.

Required documents

  • Proof of Training - Certificate from CODA-accredited program or documentation of on-the-job training hours.
  • DANB Certification - Official CDA certification from the Dental Assisting National Board, if applicable.
  • Radiography Certification - Proof of radiography competency/certification required for taking x-rays.

Key timeline factors

  • Board processing 4 to 6 Weeks
  • Fingerprinting Not required for most applicants.
  • Application completeness Missing or mismatched documents are the most common reason timelines extend.

Reciprocity

Kentucky does not require state licensure for general dental assistants. DANB national certification is recognized and recommended.

  1. STEP 1

    Create an Account on the eServices Portal

    Access the Kentucky Board of Dentistry's eServices Portal at kydental.mylicense.com/eGov/ and create a new user account, setting up your login credentials.

    Fee: $0 · Timeline: 4 to 6 Weeks

  2. STEP 2

    Complete the Online Application

    Log into the eServices Portal and complete the application for Dental Assistant Registration, providing all required personal, educational, and employment information.

    Fee: $0 · Timeline: 4 to 6 Weeks

  3. STEP 3

    Submit Required Supporting Documents

    Upload or arrange for submission of official high school or GED transcripts, official transcripts from a Board-approved dental assisting program (or DANB CDA/COA certification, or verification of two years of employment as a dental assistant), a recent photograph, and current CPR certification for Healthcare Providers.

    Fee: $0 · Timeline: 4 to 6 Weeks

  4. STEP 4

    Complete Criminal Background Check

    Follow the specific instructions provided within the eServices Portal application for completing the mandatory criminal background check.

    Fee: $0 · Timeline: 4 to 6 Weeks

  5. STEP 5

    Pay Application Fee

    Remit the non-refundable application fee of $50.00 through the eServices Portal to finalize your application submission.

    Fee: $0 · Timeline: 4 to 6 Weeks

Fees and Costs

Application fee
$0
Background check
Varies by provider
Jurisprudence exam
$0
Total estimated cost
$0

Always confirm current fees on the board site before submitting.

Board Contact

Board
Kentucky Board of Dentistry
Phone
(502) 429-7270

Application page
Open application

Renewal

Dental Assistant renewal in Kentucky follows the board cycle below.

Renewal cycle
Every 2 years (by March 1st of even-numbered years)
Renewal fee
$50
Continuing education
See board

Frequently Asked Questions

Does Kentucky require a dental assistant license?

No, Kentucky does not require state licensure for general dental assistants. However, expanded functions and radiography may require permits or certifications.

Is DANB certification recognized in Kentucky?

Yes, DANB CDA certification is recognized and often preferred by employers in Kentucky.

Can I take dental x-rays in Kentucky?

Yes, but a radiography permit or certification is required.

How much does it cost to become a dental assistant in Kentucky?

There is no state application fee for general dental assistants in Kentucky. However, DANB CDA exam costs $450, and radiography certifications may have additional fees.

At a glance

Endorsement fee

$0

Processing time

4 to 6 Weeks

Fingerprinting

Not required

Jurisprudence exam

Not required

Temporary license

Not available

National exam

Kentucky Jurisprudence Examination

Compact

No · plan on endorsement

Renewal

Every 2 years (by March 1st of even-numbered years) · $50

Licensing board

Kentucky Board of Dentistry
